channel.title: Indieweb and Webmentions
channel.description:
# Description Webmentions is a protocol for basically creating a decentralized comments section on a website.
More information about it here: https://dev.to/tcgumus/what-is-webmention-5c41
https://alistapart.com/article/webmentions-enabling-better-communication-on-the-internet/
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Webmention
# Examples https://aaronparecki.com/2020/06/28/7/blog-posts
# Possible solution I'm a designer, not a super experienced dev so I don't actually know the specifics of how webmentions work exactly but I see a wordpress specific solution here: https://github.com/pfefferle/wordpress-webmention
and I see a api project here:
https://github.com/glennjones/webmentions

item.title: Indieweb and Webmentions
item.dc:creator: thedonquixotic
item.description:
Description

Webmentions is a protocol for basically creating a decentralized comments section on a website.

More information about it here:

Pikseladam

Pikseladam - An indie blog

Pikseladam bir indie blog sitesidir. Pikseladam sitesini; makalelerimi, notlarımı, ve internette bulduğum güzel şeyleri paylaşmak için kullanıyorum.

A List Apart – 19 Jul 18

Webmentions: Enabling Better Communication on the Internet

The free exchange of information and ideas is one of the great beauties of the internet, so why is so much of that communication still trapped behind the walls of individual social silos? Enter Web…

en.wikipedia.org

Webmention

Webmention is a W3C recommendation that describes a simple protocol to notify any URL when a website links to it, and for web pages to request notifications when somebody links to them. Webmention was originally developed in the IndieWebCamp community and published as a W3C working draft on January 12, 2016. As of January 12, 2017 it is a W3C recommendation. Webmention enables authors to keep track of who is linking to, referring to, or commenting on their articles. By incorporating s Similar to ...

Examples

Aaron Parecki

Redesigning my Blog Post Pages

I had a great time in the sessions at IndieWebCamp West yesterday! Today is project day, so I started the morning off listening to some chill tunes with other folks on the Zoom "hallway track" deciding what to work on.

Possible solution

I’m a designer, not a super experienced dev so I don’t actually know the specifics of how webmentions work exactly but I see a wordpress specific solution here:

GitHub

pfefferle/wordpress-webmention

A Webmention plugin for WordPress. Contribute to pfefferle/wordpress-webmention development by creating an account on GitHub.

and I see a api project here:

GitHub

glennjones/webmentions

An API of helper function for providing and consuming webmentions - glennjones/webmentions

Read full topic
